sentences.base_language: My recipe for paella
sentences.target_language: 私のパエリアのレシピ
sentences.romanized: watashi no paeria no reshipi
sentences.literal: My paella [possessive] recipe.

sentences.base_language: One of my favorite foods is paella
sentences.target_language: 私の大好きな食べ物の一つはパエリアです
sentences.romanized: watashi no daisuki na tabemono no hitotsu wa paeria desu
sentences.literal: My most-loved [adj] food [possessive] one [topic] paella is.

sentences.base_language: Traditionally, paella is made with sea food but My recipe is vegetarian
sentences.target_language: 伝統的にパエリアは海鮮で作りますが、私のレシピはベジタリアンです
sentences.romanized: dentouteki ni paeria wa kaisen de tsukurimasu ga, watashi no reshipi wa bejitarian desu
sentences.literal: Traditionally paella [topic] seafood [means] make but, my recipe [topic] vegetarian is.

sentences.base_language: First I cut half a yellow onion,
sentences.target_language: まず黄色い玉ねぎを半分切ります
sentences.romanized: mazu kiiroi tamanegi wo hanbun kirimasu
sentences.literal: First yellow onion [obj] half cut.

sentences.base_language: I mince four cloves of garlic,
sentences.target_language: にんにくを四片みじん切りにします
sentences.romanized: ninniku wo yonhen mijingiri ni shimasu
sentences.literal: Garlic [obj] four-cloves mince-cut into do.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜にします (ni shimasu) means 'to make it into [something]' or 'to do [something] to a state'. みじん切りにします = 'I make it into minced pieces'. This pattern is used for cooking transformations.

sentences.base_language: And I chop 2 small carrots and a bell pepper
sentences.target_language: そして小さい人参二本とパプリカを切ります
sentences.romanized: soshite chiisai ninjin nihon to papurika wo kirimasu
sentences.literal: And small carrots two-[long-objects] and bell pepper [obj] cut.

sentences.base_language: Everything is cooked in one pan so I start heating up some oil on the stove
sentences.target_language: 全部一つのフライパンで調理するので、まずコンロで油を温めます
sentences.romanized: zenbu hitotsu no furaipan de chouri suru node, mazu konro de abura wo atatamemasu
sentences.literal: Everything one [possessive] frying-pan [means] cook because, first stove [location] oil [obj] warm.
sentences.grammarNote: で (de) has two uses here: フライパンで means 'using a frying pan' (instrument), and コンロで means 'on the stove' (location of action). Context determines which meaning applies.

sentences.base_language: Cook the onion and garlic until the onion is translucent
sentences.target_language: 玉ねぎが透明になるまで玉ねぎとにんにくを炒めます
sentences.romanized: tamanegi ga toumei ni naru made tamanegi to ninniku wo itamemasu
sentences.literal: Onion [subj] transparent become until onion and garlic [obj] stir-fry.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜まで (made) means 'until'. 透明になるまで = 'until it becomes transparent'. When following a verb in plain form, まで sets a time limit for the action.

sentences.base_language: Then you add the carrots, bell pepper, and a can of artichokes
sentences.target_language: 次に人参、パプリカ、そしてアーティチョークの缶詰を加えます
sentences.romanized: tsugi ni ninjin, papurika, soshite aatiichooku no kanzume wo kuwaemasu
sentences.literal: Next carrots, bell pepper, and artichoke [possessive] canned-goods [obj] add.

sentences.base_language: After ten minutes add half a can of tomato paste
sentences.target_language: 十分後にトマトペーストを半缶加えます
sentences.romanized: juppun go ni tomato peesuto wo hankan kuwaemasu
sentences.literal: Ten-minutes after [direction] tomato paste [obj] half-can add.

sentences.base_language: Season with black pepper, paprika, salt, and just a pinch of saffron (optional)
sentences.target_language: 黒胡椒、パプリカパウダー、塩で味付けし、サフランを少々加えます（お好みで）
sentences.romanized: kurokoshou, papurika paudaa, shio de ajitsuke shi, safuran wo shoushou kuwaemasu (okonomi de)
sentences.literal: Black pepper, paprika powder, salt [means] season, saffron [obj] a-little add (to-taste).

sentences.base_language: Evenly cover
sentences.target_language: 均等に広げます
sentences.romanized: kintou ni hirogemasu
sentences.literal: Evenly spread.

sentences.base_language: After all that mixes in well add two and a half cups of vegeatable broth
sentences.target_language: 全部よく混ざったら、野菜ブイヨンを二杯半加えます
sentences.romanized: zenbu yoku mazattara, yasai buiyon wo nihai han kuwaemasu
sentences.literal: Everything well mixed-if, vegetable broth [obj] two-cups half add.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜たら (tara) is a conditional meaning 'once / when [something has happened]'. 混ざったら = 'once everything has mixed well'. It's used when the first action must complete before the second happens.

sentences.base_language: If you want it to be more like traditional paella you would add chicken broth
sentences.target_language: もっと伝統的なパエリアにしたい場合は、チキンブイヨンを使います
sentences.romanized: motto dentouteki na paeria ni shitai baai wa, chikin buiyon wo tsukaimasu
sentences.literal: More traditional [adj] paella into want-to-make case [topic], chicken broth [obj] use.

sentences.base_language: Bring the broth up to a boil then add bomba rice evenly
sentences.target_language: ブイヨンを沸騰させてから、ボンバ米を均等に加えます
sentences.romanized: buiyon wo futtou sasete kara, bonba mai wo kintou ni kuwaemasu
sentences.literal: Broth [obj] boil make-do and-then, bomba rice [obj] evenly add.
sentences.grammarNote: させてから (sasete kara) combines the causative させる + てから ('after doing'). 沸騰させてから = 'after making it boil'. てから emphasizes that the first action must fully complete before the second begins.

sentences.base_language: Cook on high for 5 minutes
sentences.target_language: 強火で五分間調理します
sentences.romanized: tsuyobi de gofunkan chouri shimasu
sentences.literal: High-heat [means] five-minutes cook.

sentences.base_language: Cook on medium for 5 minutes
sentences.target_language: 中火で五分間調理します
sentences.romanized: chuubi de gofunkan chouri shimasu
sentences.literal: Medium-heat [means] five-minutes cook.

sentences.base_language: Let the paella cook for another fifteen minutes on low (gently simmer)
sentences.target_language: 弱火でさらに十五分間パエリアを煮込みます（コトコト煮る）
sentences.romanized: yowabi de sara ni juugofunkan paeria wo nikomimasu (kotokoto niru)
sentences.literal: Low-heat [means] further fifteen-minutes paella [obj] simmer (gently simmer).

sentences.base_language: You can add shrimp at this stage if you want some sort of meat
sentences.target_language: 肉を入れたい場合は、この段階でエビを加えることができます
sentences.romanized: niku wo iretai baai wa, kono dankai de ebi wo kuwaeru koto ga dekimasu
sentences.literal: Meat [obj] want-to-add case [topic], this stage [location] shrimp [obj] add [nominalizer] [subj] can-do.

sentences.base_language: I like to burn it a little at the end by going back to high for one minute
sentences.target_language: 最後に強火で一分間焼いて、少し焦げ目をつけるのが好きです
sentences.romanized: saigo ni tsuyobi de ippunkan yaite, sukoshi kogeme wo tsukeru no ga suki desu
sentences.literal: At-end high-heat [means] one-minute cook, a-little char [obj] attach [nominalizer] [subj] like is.

sentences.base_language: Take off the stove, cover and let rest for ten minutes
sentences.target_language: 火から下ろして、蓋をして十分間休ませます
sentences.romanized: hi kara oroshite, futa wo shite juppunkan yasumasemasu
sentences.literal: Fire from remove-and, lid [obj] do-and ten-minutes rest-let.

sentences.base_language: And that's it, a pretty good paella recipe for 4 people
sentences.target_language: 以上です、四人分のかなり美味しいパエリアのレシピです
sentences.romanized: ijou desu, yonin bun no kanari oishii paeria no reshipi desu
sentences.literal: That-is-all, four-people worth [possessive] pretty delicious paella [possessive] recipe is.

sentences.base_language: Let me know if you like it!
sentences.target_language: 気に入ったら教えてください！
sentences.romanized: ki ni ittara oshiete kudasai!
sentences.literal: Take-a-liking-to-if tell please!
